title: Golf trolley
abstract: The invention is directed to golf trolley (100) for carrying of a golf bag (200). In an operational state the golf trolley comprises a left front wheel (110) being arranged on a horizontal front axis (130) and driven by a left motor (111) and the horizontal front axis (130) constitutes a first horizontal plane (Q). According to the invention a right front wheel (120) is arranged on the front axis (130) and driven by a right motor (121). A rear wheel (140) is arranged a distance apart from the front axis (130) while the left and the right front wheel (110, 120) and the rear wheel (130) constitute a forward driving direction (w) of the golf trolley (100). A base structure (150) mechanically interconnects the left front wheel (110) and the right front wheel (120) and the rear wheel (130). According to the invention a pillar (160) is mechanically interconnected with the base structure (150) in the vicinity of the front axis (130) and is configured to be interconnected with a golf bag (200) to be conveyed by the golf trolley (100). The pillar (160) protrudes from the base structure (150) in direction of the rear wheel (140) by a tilt angle (Î±) of between 35Â° and 85Â° from the first horizontal plane (Q).
claims:
Claims 1. A golf trolley ( 100) for carrying of a golf bag (200), in an operational state of the golf trolley ( 100) comprising a. a left front wheel (110) being arranged on a horizontal front axis (130) and driven by a left motor (111); and b. the horizontal front axis ( 130) constituting a first horizontal plane (Q); and c. a right front wheel (120) being arranged on the front axis ( 130) and driven by a right motor (121); and d. a rear wheel (140) being arranged a distance apart from the front axis (130) e. the left and the right front wheel (110, 120) and the rear wheel ( 130) constituting a forward driving direction (w) of the golf trolley (100); and f. a base structure (150) mechanically interconnecting the left front wheel (110) and the right front wheel (120) and the rearwheel (130); and g. a pillar ( 160) attached to the base structure ( 150) and configured to be interconnected with a golf bag (200) to be conveyed by the golf trolley ( 100), the pillar ( 160) protruding from the base structure ( 150) in direc tion of the rearwheel (140) by a tilt angle (a) of between 35Â° and 85Â° from the first horizontal plane (Q). 2. The golf trolley ( 100) according to claim 1 , wherein the pillar (160) can be pivoted about a first fold axis (161), the first fold axis (161) being parallel to the front axis (130). 3. The golf trolley (100) according to claim 2, wherein the first fold axis (161) is ar- ranged at a certain distance apart from the front axis (130). 4. The golf trolley ( 100) according to any one of claims 2 and 3, wherein in an oper ational state of the golf trolley (100) the first fold axis (161) is arranged vertically above the front axis (130). 5. The golf trolley ( 100) according to any one of the claims 2 to 4, wherein a centre- piece (156) is arranged at the base structure (150) interconnecting the base struc ture ( 150) with the pillar ( 160), the front axis (130) passing and the first fold axis (161) passing through the centrepiece (156). 6. The golf trolley (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein a han dle (180) is interconnected with the pillar ( 160) in order to control driving of the golf trolley (100). 7. The golf trolley (100) according to claim 6, wherein the handle ( 180) is intercon nected with the pillar ( 160) by an extension means (170). 8. The golf trolley ( 100) according to any one of claims 6 and 7, wherein in an oper ational state of the golf trolley ( 100) the handle ( 180) in direction of the forward driving direction (w) is located behind the rear wheel ( 140). 9. The golf trolley ( 100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein a first coupling means (310) is arranged at the pillar (160) configured to attach a golf bag (200) such that the golf bag aligns with the pillar (160). 10. The golf trolley ( 100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the base structure (150) comprises a front axle (151) configured to interconnect the left front wheel (110) with the right front wheel ( 130) when the golf trolley (100) is in an operational state. 11. The golf trolley (100) according to claim 10, wherein the front axle (151) com prises a left pivoting member (152) being pivotable about a left pivot axis (132) from an operational state to a collapsed state and a right pivoting member (153) being pivotable about a right pivot axis (133) from an operational state to a col lapsed state. 12. The golf trolley (100) according to claim 11 , wherein the left motor (111) is ar ranged at the left pivot member (152) and the right motor ( 112) is arranged at the right pivot member ( 153). 13. The golf trolley (100) according to claim 12, wherein the left motor (111) is ar ranged within the left pivot member (152) and the right motor (121) is arranged within the right pivot member (153). 14. The golf trolley (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the left front wheel (110) can be disconnected from a left wheel hub (113) arranged at the left pivoting member (152) and the right front wheel (120) can be disconnected from a right wheel hub (123) arranged at the base structure (150). 15. The golf trolley (100) according to any one of claims 11 to 14, wherein pivoting of the left pivoting member (152) can be restricted by a left locking member ( 154) and pivoting of the right pivoting member (153) can be restricted by the right locking member ( 155). 16. The golf trolley ( 100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the rear wheel (140) is pivotably attached to the base structure (150) such that the run ning direction (w) of the rear wheel ( 140) is rotatable about a steering axis (141). 17. The golf trolley ( 100) according to claim 16, wherein the golf trolley ( 100) com prises a rotation prevention means (142) configured to restrain the rear wheel (140) at least temporarily from rotating about the steering axis (141). 18. The golf trolley ( 100) according to one of claims 16 and 17, wherein the rear wheel ( 140) is held by a rear wheel mount (145) that is pivotably attached to the base structure ( 150) and a first restraint member (143) arranged at the rear wheel mount (145) and a complementary second restraint member ( 144) arranged at the base structure (150), the first and the second restraint members (143, 144) being at tracted to each other by a magnetic force and thereby aligning the rear wheel (140) to a fixed running direction. 19. The golf trolley (100) according to claim 18, wherein the second restraint member (144) can be moved away from the first restraint member (143) by an unlocking device (146) in order to reduce the magnetic force and hence allow rotations of the rear wheel ( 140) about the steering axis (141). 20. The golf trolley ( 1 00) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the golf trolley ( 1 00) comprises an electrical interface ( 1 90) arranged to be intercon nected with a power supply device ( 1 91 ). 21. The golf trolley ( 1 00) according to claim 20, wherein the electrical interface ( 1 90) is arranged in the vicinity of the front axis ( 1 30). 22. The golf trolley ( 1 00) according to any one of claims 20 and 21 , wherein the pillar ( 1 60) comprises a first and a second rod ( 1 62, 1 63 ) arranged a distance apart from each other and the electrical interface ( 1 90) being arranged between the first and the second rod ( 1 62, 1 63 ). 23. A coupling assembly (300) for interconnecting a golf bag ( 200) with a golf trolley ( 1 00), comprising a. a first coupling means (31 0) comprising a first connector means ( 31 1 ) configured to be interconnected with a golf trolley ( 1 00) b. a second coupling means (320) configured to be interconnected with a golf bag ( 200) c. the first and the second coupling means (31 0, 320) being detachably in- terconnectable with each other. 24. The coupling assembly (300) according to claim 23, wherein the first coupling means (31 0) comprises an upper first coupling member (31 1 ) and a lower first cou pling member (3 1 2) and the second coupling means (320) comprising an upper second coupling member (321 ) to be interconnected with the upper first coupling 

 member ( 31 1 ) and a lower second coupling member ( 322) to be interconnected with the lower first coupling member ( 31 2), the upper first coupling member (3 1 1 ) and /or the lower first coupling member (31 2) to be attached to the pillar ( 1 60) of a golf trolley ( 1 00). 25. The coupling assembly (300) according to one of claims 23 and 24, wherein the second coupling means ( 320) is configured to be permanently interconnected with a golf bag (200). 26. The coupling assembly (300) according to one of claims 23 and 24, wherein the second coupling means ( 320) is configured to be detachably interconnected with a golf bag ( 200). 27. The coupling assembly (300) according to claim 24, wherein the upper second cou pling member (321 ) and/or the lower second coupling member ( 322) comprises a strap (323 ) arranged to be at least partially wound around the golf bag (200) and thereby securing the golf bag (200). 28. The coupling assembly (300) according to claim 24, wherein the upper second cou pling member (321 ) and the lower second coupling member ( 322) are mechanically interconnected with each by an alignment means (325) that facilitates correct posi tioning of the upper second coupling member (321 ) and a lower second coupling member (322 ) relatively to each other even if not interconnected with a golf bag ( 1 00) .
